title: Review & Setlist: ‘We are foreign imports, so we have paid tariffs.’
author: Marc Hirsh
bullets: The Hives performed at the House of Blues with Australian band The Chats as opening act on March 20, 2026.
Frontman Howlin' Pelle Almqvist engaged the audience with dynamic performances and lively interactions during the concert.
The setlist included fan favorites like 'Hate To Say I Told You So' and new tracks like 'Enough Is Enough.'

title: Cancer couldn’t stop her. Now she’s running the Boston Marathon again.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Stephanie McHugh, 42, from Braintree, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for the New England Patriots Foundation.
Diagnosed with Stage 3 breast cancer in 2023, McHugh overcame treatment challenges and returned to running.
McHugh's participation honors friend Susan Hurley and her late uncle, retired Fire Chief Kenny McHugh.

title: Markey presses Meta on facial recognition risks in smart glasses
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Sen. Ed Markey warns Meta about privacy risks of facial recognition in smart glasses, sent letter on March 17.
Markey, Wyden, and Merkley urge Meta to address potential misuse of tech for tracking without consent.
Meta has until April 6 to clarify data handling and opt-out options for proposed facial recognition use.

title: Debate in Quincy over funding for new firefighting gear after current gear tests positive for PFAS
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Quincy city council delays vote on $2.65 million bond to replace firefighter gear containing PFAS chemicals.
Mayor Thomas Koch urges council to fund PFAS-free turnout gear due to health risks like cancer linked to chemicals.
Council seeks more time to review gear proposal; firefighters express frustration over continued exposure risks.

title: The battle over the aging Attleboro Zoo is getting wild
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Attleboro proposes converting Capron Park Zoo into a nature reserve to address rising costs and infrastructure issues.
The zoo requires $57 million in repairs and costs the city $900,000 annually amid declining attendance.
A transition team including city leaders and zoo representatives will form in July to manage the change.

title: What does it mean to have a partner?
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Watertown couple Abigail Brown and Colin Wong organized a last-minute microwedding due to a blizzard on January 26, 2026.
FX's 'Love Story' series about John F. Kennedy Jr. and Carolyn Bessette explores 1990s communication nostalgia.
Debate arises over the term 'partner' in relationships, questioning its use for short-term or long-distance dating.

title: After loss and cancer, a Burlington runner keeps moving toward Boston
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Adam Averback, from Burlington, is running the Boston Marathon with Team MGH to honor his late father.
Averback lost his father to glioblastoma and survived testicular cancer, reshaping his outlook on life.
After overcoming a serious blood infection, Averback emphasizes running as a tribute to endurance and survival.

title: New Patriots S Kevin Byard working out with rumored New England trade target A.J. Brown
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Patriots express interest in trading for Eagles WR A.J. Brown, reportedly making an offer declined by Philadelphia.
Brown, a five-time 1,000-yard receiver, could be traded post-June 1 for salary cap reasons.
Kevin Byard, Patriots safety, working out with Brown, fueling trade speculation to New England.

title: James Hagens watch begins as Bruins brace for top prospect’s potential arrival
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens ends his Boston College sophomore season with 47 points in 34 games, signaling a potential move to the Bruins.
Boston Bruins GM Don Sweeney remains open to adding top prospect James Hagens to the roster for the final regular-season stretch.
Hagens, the seventh pick in the 2025 NHL Draft, is expected to provide offensive support as a potential top-six forward for the Bruins.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Is This Thing On?' starring Will Arnett is now streaming on Disney+.
'Peaky Blinders: The Immortal Man' featuring Cillian Murphy streams on Netflix.
'Born to Bowl' docuseries narrated by Liev Schreiber is available on HBO Max.
